title: Multiple competing ZakOps stacks / unclear source of truth
description: Overlapping ZakOps stacks and backend-ish codepaths create ambiguity about which service is authoritative for deals, quarantine, actions, and tool execution.

sources.quote: Key contrarian fact: you have multiple “backend-ish” codepaths in-repo, but the only backend actually serving :8091 is zakops-backend/.
There are two separate "ZakOps" systems running simultaneously.

evidence: Report A: backend observed on http://localhost:8091 built from zakops-backend/; zakops-agent-api/apps/backend exists but not serving :8091.
Report B: legacy /home/zaks/Zaks-llm/ stack with zakops-api on port 8080.
remediation_actions: Decide and document the single authoritative ZakOps runtime (which API owns deals/quarantine/actions).
Decommission, disable, or explicitly scope legacy services (e.g., zakops-api:8080) to remove ambiguity.
Remove or clearly label unused backend-looking codepaths (e.g., zakops-agent-api/apps/backend).
Align scripts/docs to the authoritative ports/services.
verification_steps: Run `docker ps` and confirm only intended ZakOps services are running.
Run `ss -lntp` and confirm only expected ports are listening.
Confirm dashboard/agent base URLs target the chosen authoritative backend.
definition_of_done: One declared authoritative API for deals/quarantine/actions; non-authoritative stacks are stopped or formally documented as legacy/bridge; onboarding docs match the runtime.
conflicts: Report A says :8091 is served by zakops-backend; Report B describes backend as part of the zakops-agent-api monorepo. Needs runtime verification of which stack is standard.

title: Port & URL configuration drift (8090/8091/9200) + docker-compose conflicts
description: Inconsistent ports across env files, Docker defaults, docs, and clients plus docker-compose port collisions prevent deterministic operation and cause misrouting.

sources.quote: Port 8090 is still referenced widely in docs/scripts/config despite V3 claiming it’s decommissioned.
Port conflict: both backend services claim 8091.

evidence: Report B: backend-orchestration mapped to 8091:8091 (same as deal-lifecycle) not 9200:9200; cannot run both from compose.
Report B: .env.example uses 8090; Dockerfile.api defaults 8090; scripts/dev.sh uses 8091; runtime is 8091 but defaults conflict.
Report B: apps/dashboard/src/lib/agent-client.ts defaults to port 9200 if NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL missing.
Report A: 8090 appears in zakops-backend/.env.example and env (ZAKOPS_DEAL_API_URL); legacy MCP server hard-coded to :8090.
remediation_actions: Fix docker-compose port mappings so lifecycle and orchestration can run without collisions (e.g., 8091 and 9200).
Normalize .env.example, Docker defaults, and dev scripts to a single port set aligned with runtime.
Fix dashboard client fallbacks (agent-client.ts) to target the correct API/port.
Remove/cordon legacy 8090 references (env/docs/tools, hard-coded MCP server).
verification_steps: Run `docker compose up -d` and confirm no port binding errors.
Run `curl http://localhost:8091/health/ready` (if present) and verify success.
Run `curl http://localhost:9200/health/ready` (if intended) and verify success.
Start dashboard without NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and confirm it does not silently call the wrong port.
definition_of_done: Compose starts intended services without collisions; examples/defaults match runtime; no active runtime path relies on 8090 unless explicitly documented as legacy.

title: Dashboard hides failures (mock endpoints + fail-open empty responses)
description: Dashboard can return empty/mock data when backend is unreachable and can show mock agent activity, masking outages and breaking operator situational awareness.

sources.quote: Several Dashboard API routes “fail open” and return empty/mock data on backend errors (pipeline, checkpoints, delete action, etc.)
If backend not running, dashboard silently returns empty arrays (graceful degradation hides failures).

evidence: Report A: mock agent activity endpoint zakops-agent-api/apps/dashboard/src/app/api/agent/activity/route.ts.
Report B: dashboard hides backend outage by returning empty arrays.
remediation_actions: Add explicit backend connectivity/health indicator in the UI.
Gate mock/fail-open behavior behind a clear demo-mode flag or remove it for production.
Surface backend errors (banner/toast) instead of silent empty arrays.
verification_steps: Stop backend and confirm UI shows 'disconnected' state rather than empty lists.
Restart backend and confirm UI returns to connected state with real data.
definition_of_done: UI differentiates 'no data' vs 'system down'; mock endpoints removed or explicitly gated; backend errors are visible to operators.

title: Deal stage taxonomy inconsistent across UI and backend
description: Multiple incompatible stage enumerations exist across UI types, backend workflow, and UI create form, causing inconsistent sorting/filtering/transitions and unclear semantics.

sources.quote: Multiple, conflicting stage models across UI + backend.

evidence: Report A: UI stages in zakops-agent-api/apps/dashboard/src/types/execution-contracts.ts.
Report A: backend stages in zakops-backend/src/core/deals/workflow.py.
Report A: UI create form stages in zakops-agent-api/apps/dashboard/src/app/deals/new/page.tsx.
remediation_actions: Choose a canonical stage taxonomy and document stage meanings.
Align backend workflow validation and all UI stage lists to the canonical taxonomy.
Migrate existing deal stage values that don't match the canonical list.
verification_steps: Verify UI dropdowns/kanban columns and backend validation accept the same stage values.
Transition multiple deals and verify resulting stages match across DB and UI.
definition_of_done: Single canonical stage taxonomy across backend and UI; no DB stage values outside the canonical set.

title: Deal transition API contract mismatch (to_stage vs new_stage)
description: Dashboard sends {to_stage, approved_by} while backend expects {new_stage}, causing UI stage transitions to fail (HTTP 400).

sources.quote: Deal stage transitions from UI: UI sends {to_stage, approved_by} but backend expects {new_stage}.

evidence: Report A: UI transitionDeal() in zakops-agent-api/apps/dashboard/src/lib/api.ts sends to_stage/approved_by.
Report A: backend expects new_stage in zakops-backend/src/api/orchestration/routers/workflow.py.
remediation_actions: Update dashboard transitionDeal() to send new_stage (or add a server-side adapter translating payloads).
Add a contract test preventing payload drift.
verification_steps: Trigger a transition from the UI and confirm 2xx (no HTTP 400).
Call `POST /api/deals/{deal_id}/transition` with {"new_stage": ...} and confirm success.
definition_of_done: Stage transitions succeed from UI and are reflected in backend events/history.

title: /api/threads/* broken (DB env var mismatch + missing schema) blocks approvals/runs UX
description: Dashboard runs/approvals UX calls backend /api/threads/*, but Report A says these endpoints 500 due to missing ZAKOPS_DATABASE_URL and missing zakops.agent_threads table.

sources.quote: Backend /api/threads is wired to a DB URL env var that is not set + schema not present.

evidence: Report A: zakops-backend/src/api/orchestration/agent_invocation.py uses ZAKOPS_DATABASE_URL (not DATABASE_URL).
Report A: backend container does not set ZAKOPS_DATABASE_URL; endpoints 500.
Report A: missing zakops.agent_threads migration/table.
remediation_actions: Decide whether backend /api/threads is intended; either fix it or remove it and rewire the dashboard to the working approvals system.
If keeping: standardize on DATABASE_URL (or set ZAKOPS_DATABASE_URL consistently) and add the missing zakops.agent_threads migration.
If removing: delete endpoints and update dashboard code that calls /api/threads/*.
verification_steps: Call the /api/threads/* endpoints used by the UI and confirm 2xx (no 500).
End-to-end: create an approval from the agent, approve in UI, and confirm graph resumes.
definition_of_done: /api/threads/* no longer returns 500 in the intended runtime; approvals/runs UX works end-to-end against the chosen authoritative approvals implementation.
conflicts: Report B asserts HITL approvals work (agent-api flow) while Report A asserts dashboard approvals wired to backend /api/threads are dead. Requires verification and a single chosen approvals system.

title: Agent approvals DB functions not auto-applied (001_approvals.sql)
description: Agent API relies on PL/pgSQL functions for approval recovery, but these live in apps/agent-api/migrations/001_approvals.sql and must be manually applied.

sources.quote: Migration SQL not auto-executed… functions cleanup_expired_approvals, reclaim_stale_claims… must be manually applied.

evidence: Report B: apps/agent-api/migrations/001_approvals.sql contains required PL/pgSQL functions.
Report B: HITL recovery depends on these functions.
remediation_actions: Apply apps/agent-api/migrations/001_approvals.sql to the actual Agent API database.
Automate applying this SQL during deployments/service startup.
verification_steps: Query DB to confirm cleanup_expired_approvals and reclaim_stale_claims exist.
Exercise approval workflow and validate stale claim recovery behavior.
definition_of_done: Required PL/pgSQL functions exist in the correct DB across environments; approval recovery works after restarts/crashes.

title: Backend search endpoints crash due to schema mismatch (/api/search/actions, /api/search/global)
description: Backend search routers reference non-existent columns (actions.inputs, actions.risk_level) and crash with 500s; schema uses agent_config JSON instead.

sources.quote: Queries reference zakops.actions.inputs and zakops.actions.risk_level, but table uses agent_config and stores risk inside JSON.

evidence: Report A: zakops-backend/src/api/orchestration/routers/search.py.
Report A: /api/search/actions and /api/search/global throw 500.
remediation_actions: Update search.py queries to match current actions schema (use agent_config JSON extraction).
Add regression tests for /api/search/actions and /api/search/global.
verification_steps: Call /api/search/actions and confirm 200 (no 500).
Call /api/search/global and confirm 200 (no 500).
definition_of_done: Search endpoints return valid responses against current DB schema without 500 errors.

title: LLM + integration configuration brittle (wrong base URL, missing secrets, fragile fallback)
description: LLM/integration env uses localhost inside containers, keys are empty/placeholders, and provider fallback can lead to 'All providers failed' errors when vLLM is down.

sources.quote: OPENAI_API_BASE=http://localhost:8000/v1 (wrong inside container; should use a reachable host/alias).
If vLLM is down, circular fallback tries OpenAI (needs API key)… All providers failed.

evidence: Report A: missing/placeholder values for LANGSMITH_API_KEY, Gmail OAuth client id/secret, TOKEN_ENCRYPTION_KEY; incorrect OPENAI_API_BASE inside container.
Report B: executors depend on vLLM; failure mode when vLLM down triggers OpenAI fallback requiring API key.
remediation_actions: Fix container-to-container LLM base URL (do not use localhost inside containers for vLLM).
Provide required secrets/keys or explicitly disable integrations not in use (LangSmith, Gmail OAuth, encryption key).
Make provider fallback policy explicit (disable OpenAI fallback if no key; return clear error when vLLM unavailable).
Add startup-time env validation (fail fast when required config is missing).
verification_steps: With vLLM up: run a tool/executor and confirm success.
With vLLM down: confirm deterministic behavior (expected fallback or clear error), not circular provider attempts.
definition_of_done: No localhost cross-container LLM calls; required secrets present or integrations disabled; deterministic and observable provider failure behavior.

title: Email/DataRoom → Quarantine → Deal bridge missing (filesystem→DB ingestion gap)
description: Inbound email/files may land in DataRoom or email endpoints exist, but there is no reliable automated bridge populating quarantine_items in Postgres for operator triage and deal creation.

sources.quote: No evidence of a running email ingestion loop populating quarantine.
No automatic bridge from filesystem → quarantine_items DB table.

evidence: Report A: backend exposes /api/integrations/email/* and /api/quarantine*; table zakops.quarantine_items exists.
Report B: sync_acquisition_emails.py writes to /home/zaks/DataRoom/00-PIPELINE/Inbound/{deal}/; backend in docker does not mount DataRoom.
remediation_actions: Choose ingestion strategy (DB-first or filesystem-first) and document it.
If filesystem-first: add DataRoom volume mounts to backend container and implement a bridge job that creates quarantine_items from inbound files.
Operationalize ingestion (poller/webhook) to populate zakops.inbox and zakops.quarantine_items.
Add ingestion observability (processed counts, failures, quarantine created).
verification_steps: Send test email with attachment and confirm a new quarantine_items row appears and is visible in /quarantine UI.
Approve the item and confirm a deal record is created.
definition_of_done: Inbound email/files automatically create quarantine items; operator can approve/reject and see deterministic results (deal created or rejected) without manual DB inserts.

title: Quarantine UI/back-end contract mismatch (UI treats quarantine as actions; backend exposes /api/quarantine)
description: Dashboard quarantine page assumes quarantine items are actions and calls /api/actions/{id}/execute, but backend quarantine is a different model and action execution endpoints are not exposed (per Report A).

sources.quote: Quarantine page assumes “quarantine items are actions” and tries POST /api/actions/{id}/execute… backend quarantine is a different model (/api/quarantine/*).

evidence: Report A: zakops-agent-api/apps/dashboard/src/app/quarantine/page.tsx calls /api/actions/{id}/execute.
Report A: backend quarantine endpoints exist under /api/quarantine/*.
remediation_actions: Pick canonical quarantine model: first-class quarantine_items (/api/quarantine/*) OR action-based quarantine.
Align dashboard quarantine page to canonical backend contract; remove calls to non-existent endpoints.
Add end-to-end test: create quarantine item → approve → expected deal/action result.
verification_steps: Approve/reject a quarantine item from UI and confirm backend processes it without 4xx/5xx.
Confirm resulting DB state change and UI updates.
definition_of_done: Quarantine approve/reject works end-to-end; UI only calls implemented endpoints; canonical quarantine model is documented.

title: Actions/outbox processing not running end-to-end (empty actions table, missing worker)
description: Action execution depends on background workers (outbox processor and/or actions_runner.py), but workers are not running in the observed stack and action pipeline is not demonstrably functioning.

sources.quote: The actions table is currently empty… outbox worker… not running right now… /health/ready returns outbox_processor: not running.
actions_runner.py must be running as separate process.

evidence: Report A: zakops-backend/docker-compose.yml defines outbox-worker but it is not running; /health/ready indicates outbox_processor not running; actions table empty.
Report B: actions_runner.py must run as separate process/container; in dev must be started manually.
remediation_actions: Ensure required worker(s) run by default in the standard compose/dev workflow; make /health/ready green for processors.
Define and document a single action lifecycle contract (create, lease, execute, retry, terminal states).
Add a smoke test: create action → observe lease acquisition → observe completion/failure status.
verification_steps: Create a test action and confirm DB row appears and worker transitions status to a terminal state.
Check /health/ready (if present) reports processors running.
definition_of_done: Workers run by default; actions created by UI/agent execute reliably and reach terminal states; health checks reflect running processors.

title: Chat proposal execution stubbed (HTTP 501)
description: Dashboard route /api/chat/execute-proposal is explicitly unimplemented and returns 501, blocking 'approve from chat' execution.

sources.quote: Chat proposal execution is explicitly unimplemented… always returns 501 (placeholder).

evidence: Report A: zakops-agent-api/apps/dashboard/src/app/api/chat/execute-proposal/route.ts returns 501 placeholder.
remediation_actions: Implement execute-proposal to trigger the corresponding backend action/quarantine operation, or remove the UI affordance until supported.
Add an end-to-end test verifying proposal approval triggers execution.
verification_steps: Call POST /api/chat/execute-proposal and confirm 2xx and real execution (or feature removed).
definition_of_done: Proposals can be executed successfully from chat, or the feature is removed/disabled with clear messaging.

title: Backend /api/agent/invoke deal_id type mismatch (expects UUID; real IDs are strings)
description: Chat fallback calls /api/agent/invoke but backend expects UUID deal_id while real deal IDs are strings (e.g., DL-0012).

sources.quote: backend expects UUID deal_id; real deal ids are strings like DL-0012.

evidence: Report A: chat route fallback uses /api/agent/invoke; deal IDs are strings like DL-0012.
remediation_actions: Accept the real deal ID format in /api/agent/invoke or translate string IDs to internal UUIDs if present.
Add contract tests ensuring /api/agent/invoke supports the system's deal ID format.
verification_steps: Invoke /api/agent/invoke with a DL-#### deal_id and confirm success.
definition_of_done: Chat fallback works with real deal IDs used by the system.

title: Redis configured but unused (remove or implement intentionally)
description: Redis is included in compose/env but Report B states it is not used by backend/agent code; architecture/docs claims about Redis may be false.

sources.quote: Redis runs but is NOT actually used in application code.

evidence: Report B: docker-compose includes Redis and REDIS_URL env, but no Redis client usage in Python backend/agent code.
remediation_actions: Decide: remove Redis from compose/docs OR implement Redis-backed caching/session/queue features intentionally.
If removed, update docs that claim Redis supports sessions/queues/cache.
verification_steps: Code audit: confirm whether any Redis client is used.
If removed: run stack and confirm no missing Redis dependency.
If implemented: demonstrate the Redis-backed feature works and is documented.
definition_of_done: Redis is either actively used with documented responsibilities or removed from default runtime and docs.
conflicts: Report A lists Redis as running and describes backend as backed by PostgreSQL + Redis, while Report B asserts Redis is not used in code. Requires verification and a decision.

title: Database configuration sprawl & migration uncertainty (risk of wrong DB connections)
description: Multiple DBs and dangerous defaults (e.g., agent default food_order_db) can cause services to connect to or write into unintended databases; no single migration command guarantees schema correctness.

sources.quote: Backend /api/threads uses ZAKOPS_DATABASE_URL (not DATABASE_URL)… schema lacks zakops.agent_threads migration/table.
There is no single migration command that sets up all schemas correctly.

evidence: Report B: multiple DBs (zakops, zakops_agent, food_order_db, crawlrag, SQLite legacy).
Report B: agent DB default food_order_db at apps/agent-api/app/core/config.py:180.
Report A: DB env mismatch and missing tables for backend threads path.
remediation_actions: Decide authoritative DB topology (single Postgres+DB preferred) and document per-service schema usage.
Remove dangerous defaults (fix agent default DB; fail fast when env missing).
Create a unified migration entrypoint that initializes all required tables/functions for backend and agent.
Enforce DB wiring in CI (prevent drift).
verification_steps: Bring up a fresh environment and run one migration command to reach known-good schemas/functions.
Start services with minimal env and confirm they connect to intended DB(s) only (no silent defaults).
definition_of_done: Services cannot start while pointing at unintended DBs; one documented migration command provisions a fresh environment end-to-end.

title: Auth disabled by default (backend + agent) / dev-mode operator auto-created
description: Auth is disabled by default in backend and agent; dev-mode middleware creates a mock operator and no real session management is active, creating a production security risk if not explicitly gated.

sources.quote: AUTH_REQUIRED defaults to false… Auth middleware creates a mock "dev" operator… No real session management is active in dev mode.

evidence: Report B: AUTH_REQUIRED=false and AGENT_JWT_ENFORCE=false defaults; mock dev operator behavior.
remediation_actions: Create explicit dev vs prod profiles; require auth in prod with no mock operator.
Enforce AUTH_REQUIRED=true and AGENT_JWT_ENFORCE=true in production deployments.
Add CI smoke tests ensuring prod profile blocks unauthenticated requests.
verification_steps: In prod profile, call key endpoints without auth and confirm 401/403.
In dev profile, confirm local development remains usable.
definition_of_done: Production profile rejects unauthenticated access; dev profile behavior is explicitly documented and gated.

title: Hardcoded DASHBOARD_SERVICE_TOKEN in compose/.env (secret hygiene issue)
description: A dashboard service token is committed in docker-compose and .env.local, requiring removal and rotation.

sources.quote: DASHBOARD_SERVICE_TOKEN hardcoded in docker-compose… token is in the compose file and .env.local.

evidence: Report B: deployments/docker/docker-compose.yml:20,77 and .env.local contain the token.
remediation_actions: Remove the token from versioned files and rotate it.
Load the token from a secrets-only mechanism (env-only or secret manager).
Add secret scanning to prevent reintroduction.
verification_steps: Confirm token value is absent from tracked files.
Confirm rotated token works when loaded from secrets in runtime.
definition_of_done: No committed secrets in compose/.env; token rotated and sourced securely.

title: Observability stack defined but not proven working (metrics/dashboards may be stale)
description: Observability components exist (Prometheus/Grafana/Loki/Promtail; OTEL deps installed) but it's unclear whether they run and whether apps emit usable metrics/logs; dashboards may not match reality.

sources.quote: Prometheus, Grafana, Loki, and Promtail are defined in docker-compose but… Prometheus scrape targets point to services that may not expose /metrics.

evidence: Report B: ops/observability/ configs and grafana dashboards exist; OTEL instrumentation installed; Langfuse keys empty in .env.development.
remediation_actions: Verify which observability containers run in the standard stack.
Ensure services expose metrics endpoints or OTEL exporters that Prometheus can scrape.
Validate and update Grafana dashboards to match actual metrics.
Configure Langfuse/LangSmith only if required and ensure keys are present when enabled.
verification_steps: Confirm Prometheus scrape targets are up and metrics are collected.
Open Grafana dashboards and confirm live data populates.
definition_of_done: Service health and key workflow metrics are observable; dashboards and scrape targets match the running system.

title: Unverified/aspirational infra and quality gates (CI status unknown; gates/chaos/blue-green may not match runtime)
description: Readiness artifacts exist (CI workflows, gates, chaos scripts, blue/green deployments), but their validity against the current runtime is unknown or aspirational; docs also contain mismatches (agent tool count).

sources.quote: Whether they pass is unknown — likely many would fail against current runtime.

evidence: Report B: deployments/bluegreen/, tools/gates/, tools/chaos/, .github/workflows/ci.yml and deploy.yaml exist; pass/green status unknown.
Report B: docs mismatch—agent has 5 tools but V3 says 4.
remediation_actions: Run CI and record failures; fix what is required for the declared standard stack.
Run tools/gates scripts and triage failures; update scripts referencing decommissioned ports/services.
Decide if blue/green and chaos tooling are in-scope; archive/remove if not maintained.
Update docs to reflect actual agent tool count and runtime realities.
verification_steps: Run quality gates against the declared standard stack and confirm pass.
Ensure CI pipeline is green for the declared standard stack.
definition_of_done: A verified, maintained set of readiness gates exists and matches the current runtime; CI and gate results are routinely tracked.
